mission.title: localai: Allow sideloading backends on filesystem
mission.description: Allow sideloading backends on filesystem. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current localai deployment
steps.description:
Verify your localai version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n localai -l app.kubernetes.io/name=localai
helm list -n localai 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working localai installation.

steps.title: Review localai configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant localai configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n localai -l app.kubernetes.io/name=localai
kubectl get configmap -n localai -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=localai
```
I am trying to package LocalAI to archlinux, so LocalAI can be installed and run directly on archlinux with its files managed by pacman. But I find it is hard to package those backends like llama-cpp, piper, bark, etc.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow sideloading backends on filesystem
steps.description:
This PR is related to https://github.com/mudler/LocalAI/issues/5917

This allows to call `local-ai backends install` on local paths. For instance:

```
local-ai backends install path/to/backend
```

Note, to specify a backends path, set `--backends-path` accordingly, for example:

```
local-ai backends install --backends-path /var/lib/local-ai/backends path/to/backend/just/built
```

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n localai -l app.kubernetes.io/name=localai
kubectl get events -n localai --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow sideloading backends on filesystem" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This PR is related to https://github.com/mudler/LocalAI/issues/5917

This allows to call `local-ai backends install` on local paths. For instance:

```
local-ai backends install path/to/backend
```

Note, to specify a backends path, set `--backends-path` accordingly, for example:

```
local-ai backends install --backends-path /var/lib/local-ai/backends path/to/backend/just/built
```

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with localai installed or the issue environment reproducible.
